questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a salesperson candidate complete in New York?
distractors: 45 hours
90 hours
120 hours
questions.explanation: New York requires 77 hours of approved pre-license education before a salesperson candidate may sit for the licensing exam. This requirement is set by the Department of State and covers topics including law of agency, fair housing, and real property law.

questions.question_text: A 17-year-old completes the 77-hour pre-license course and passes the state exam. Can this person obtain a salesperson license?
questions.correct_answer: No, applicants must be at least 18 years old
distractors: Yes, if a parent co-signs the application
Yes, passing the exam satisfies all requirements
No, the minimum age is 21 in New York
questions.explanation: New York requires all license applicants to be at least 18 years of age. Passing the exam and completing coursework alone are insufficient if the age requirement is not met. There is no parental consent exception.

questions.question_text: The New York salesperson licensing exam contains how many questions, and what score is needed to pass?
distractors: 100 questions with a 75% passing score
80 questions with a 70% passing score
75 questions with a 75% passing score
questions.explanation: The New York salesperson exam consists of 75 multiple-choice questions. Candidates must score at least 70% (approximately 53 correct answers) to pass. The exam tests knowledge of both national and state-specific real estate topics.

questions.question_text: Maria passes her salesperson exam but has not yet found a sponsoring broker. Can she submit her license application to the DOS?
questions.correct_answer: No, a sponsoring broker is required to apply
distractors: Yes, she has 90 days to find a sponsor after applying
Yes, the DOS issues an inactive license until sponsorship
No, but she may work as an unlicensed assistant
questions.explanation: New York requires a sponsoring broker before a salesperson license application can be submitted to the DOS. Without broker sponsorship, the candidate cannot be licensed or perform any licensed activities.

questions.question_text: What are the current DOS fees for a New York salesperson license application and exam?
distractors: $100 application fee and $25 exam fee
$50 application fee and $50 exam fee
$75 combined application and exam fee
questions.explanation: The DOS charges a $55 application fee and a $15 exam fee for salesperson licensure. These fees are set by statute and are non-refundable regardless of whether the candidate passes the exam.

questions.question_text: How many continuing education hours must a New York licensee complete every two-year renewal cycle?
distractors: 15 hours
24 hours
30 hours
questions.explanation: New York requires 22.5 hours of continuing education every two years for license renewal. This total must include mandatory modules in fair housing, agency, and ethics, with the remaining hours in DOS-approved elective topics.

questions.question_text: A licensee completes 22.5 hours of CE but skips the fair housing module. Will the DOS accept this for renewal?
questions.correct_answer: No, the 3-hour fair housing module is mandatory
distractors: Yes, the total hours are all that matter
Yes, fair housing is only required for brokers
No, but a waiver is available for experienced agents
questions.explanation: The DOS requires specific mandatory modules within the 22.5 CE hours: 3 hours of fair housing, 2 hours of agency, and 1 hour of ethics. Meeting the total hour count without completing these required modules will result in rejection of the renewal application.

questions.question_text: Which mandatory continuing education topic requires the most hours per renewal cycle in New York?
distractors: Agency disclosure at 3 hours
Ethics at 2 hours
Contract law at 4 hours
questions.explanation: Fair housing requires 3 hours per cycle, making it the largest mandatory CE component. Agency requires 2 hours and ethics requires 1 hour. The remaining 16.5 hours may be fulfilled through DOS-approved elective courses.

questions.question_text: A broker completes all CE hours but uses a course provider not approved by the DOS. What is the likely outcome at renewal?
questions.correct_answer: The hours will not count toward renewal
distractors: The DOS will accept hours from any accredited school
The broker receives a 90-day extension to retake courses
Only the elective hours are rejected; mandatory hours stand
questions.explanation: All continuing education courses must be taken through DOS-approved providers. Hours from unapproved providers do not satisfy renewal requirements, and the licensee risks lapsing their license if compliant hours are not completed before the deadline.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a required continuing education topic for every New York license renewal?
distractors: Property management
Commercial leasing
Environmental hazards
questions.explanation: Ethics is one of three mandatory CE topics in New York, requiring 1 hour per renewal cycle. Property management, commercial leasing, and environmental hazards may be taken as electives but are not mandatory for renewal.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves Broker A to join Broker B. Who is responsible for notifying the DOS of the termination?
questions.correct_answer: Broker A must notify the DOS
distractors: The salesperson files the termination notice
Broker B files on behalf of both parties
No notification is required if done within 30 days
questions.explanation: When a salesperson leaves a brokerage, the terminating broker (Broker A) is responsible for notifying the DOS. The new broker (Broker B) then files the change of association to activate the salesperson under their supervision.

questions.question_text: Can a New York salesperson be actively associated with two brokers simultaneously?
distractors: Yes, with written consent from both brokers
Yes, if the brokers operate in different counties
Only if the salesperson holds an associate broker license
questions.explanation: New York prohibits dual employment for salespersons. A licensee may only be associated with one broker at a time. To change brokers, the current association must be terminated before a new one can be filed.

questions.question_text: An out-of-state broker wishes to conduct real estate transactions in New York. What must this broker file with the DOS?
questions.correct_answer: An irrevocable consent to service of process
distractors: A reciprocity application with their home state license
A temporary 90-day practice permit
A surety bond equal to their annual commission income
questions.explanation: Out-of-state brokers must file an irrevocable consent form with the DOS, designating the Secretary of State as their agent for service of process. This ensures New York courts have jurisdiction over the broker for any disputes arising from transactions in the state.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's broker closes the firm unexpectedly. The salesperson has a pending deal. What should the salesperson do first?
questions.correct_answer: Associate with a new broker before continuing the deal
distractors: Complete the deal independently to protect the client
File a temporary solo license with the DOS
Transfer the listing to any willing broker without consent
questions.explanation: A salesperson cannot conduct any licensed activity without an active broker association. When a broker closes, the salesperson must find and associate with a new broker through proper DOS filings before continuing any transactions.

questions.question_text: When a salesperson changes from Broker A to Broker B, what filing must Broker B submit to the DOS?
distractors: A new license application with exam results
An amended sponsorship affidavit from Broker A
A transfer of escrow accounts form
questions.explanation: Broker B must file a change of association application with the DOS to activate the salesperson under their supervision. The salesperson does not need to retake the exam or submit a new license application for a change of brokerage.
